document.id: Pueblo de San Ildefonso Council Res. No. SI-R26-008
document.heading: PUEBLO DE SAN ILDEFONSO COUNCIL RESOLUTION NO. SI-R26-008
Third Party Billing Authorization Act
document.preamble: At a duly called meeting of the Council of San Ildefonso Pueblo on the 10th day of June, 2026, the following resolution was adopted:

recital.text: the Pueblo de San Ildefonso ("Pueblo") is a sovereign and self-governing Tribe, recognized as such by the United States of America; and
the sovereign authority of the Pueblo is vested in its Council and Governor; and
the Pueblo formally established its Health and Human Services Department via Part 14.1.1.010 of the Pueblo de San Ildefonso Code, and Council Resolution No. SI-R12-022, dated June 18, 2012; and,

the Council enacted SI-R25-007, Part 6.5.39, "The P.L 93-638 Contract Authorization Act", which authorizes the Governor "to negotiate and execute a contract agreement and to take any other actions necessary to effectuate a contract for the following BIA-contracted, IHS-contracted, and other federal programs including but not limited to:

para.text: Consolidated Tribal Government Program
Indian Child Welfare Act Program
Johnson O'Malley Program
Scholarships Program
Community Health Representatives
Alcohol and Substance Abuse, and
Any other IHS PFSA that is eligible to contract by the Pueblo in future contracts";

recital.text: the Council acknowledges the 2023 New Mexico State Plan Amendment ("SPA") 23-0011 and SPA 21-0014, allowing Medicaid reimbursement for services of certified Community Health Workers and Representatives and Non-Emergency Transportation; and,

the HHSD provides various health services to beneficiaries under the Community Health Worker/Representative program and to eligible community members under the Behavioral Health program through its identified behavioral health provider; and,
the HHSD will begin providing Non-Emergency Transport services, prevention services, and enhancing the community's access to behavioral health services; and,
the Council recognizes that future and existing services are considered "billable services" by third-party payers, including Medicaid and authorized Managed Healthcare Organizations; and,
the Council acknowledges the benefits to the Pueblo of engaging in third-party billing for providing critical health services; and,

the Council acknowledges all Federal and State regulations associated with implementing third party billing, including the requirement to reinvest all third party revenue back into health programs for support and enhanced services as found in 25 U.S.C. § 5325(m)1.
that the Council hereby enacts the attached resolution entitled the "THIRD PARTY BILLING AUTHORIZATION ACT".

procedural-data.text: The foregoing resolution was duly voted upon by the Council on June 10, 2026, at a meeting with a vote of 8 in favor, 0 opposed, 1 abstaining and 1 absent (Governor presiding and not voting) pursuant to the authority vested in the Council by the Agreement of the Pueblo de San Ildefonso.

text.strong: DELIVERY OF THE RESOLUTION TO THE GOVERNOR:

procedural-data.text: Resolution No. SI-R26-008 was presented to the Governor of the Pueblo de San Ildefonso on the

11th day of June, 2026, pursuant to the governing Agreement of the Pueblo de San Ildefonso ("Agreement"), GOVERNOR, Sec. 4(c), and will become effective after signature by the Governor or veto override by the Council.
{ } VETO - RETURNED TO COUNCIL WITH EXPLANATION:
Presented by the Governor to the Council on the _______ day of ________________, 2026.

The foregoing resolution was duly voted upon by the Council on _________________, 2026, at a meeting with a vote of ______ in favor, _____ opposed, _____ abstaining and _____ absent (Governor presiding and not voting) pursuant to the authority vested in the Council by the Agreement of the Pueblo de San Ildefonso.

section.heading: Short Title
section.text: This enactment shall be known as the "Third Party Billing Authorization Act" ("Act")

section.heading: Purpose
section.text: The purpose of this Act is to authorize all programs of the Health and Human Services Department ("HHSD") to engage in third party billing for all eligible services, including services provided through Community Health Worker/Representative program, Non-Emergency Transportation, and Behavioral Health services.

section.heading: Findings
section.text: The Council hereby finds and declares that:

para.text: The Pueblo formally established its Health and Human Services Department via Part 14.1.1.010 of the Pueblo de San Ildefonso Code, and Council Resolution No. SI-R12-022, dated June 18, 2012; and,

The Council enacted SI-R25-007, Part 6.5.39, "The P.L 93-638 Contract Authorization Act", which authorizes the Governor "to negotiate and execute a contract agreement and to take any other actions necessary to effectuate a contract for the following BIA- contracted, IHS-contracted, and other federal programs including but not limited to:
Consolidated Tribal Government Program
Indian Child Welfare Act Program
Johnson O'Malley Program
Scholarships Program
Community Health Representatives
Alcohol and Substance Abuse, and
Any other IHS PFSA that is eligible to contract by the Pueblo in future contracts"

The Council acknowledges the 2023 New Mexico State Plan Amendment ("SPA") 23- 0011 and SPA 21-0014, allowing Medicaid reimbursement for services of certified Community Health Workers and Representatives and Non-Emergency Transportation.

The HHSD provides various health services to beneficiaries under the Community Health Worker/Representative program and to eligible community members under the Behavioral Health program through its identified behavioral health provider.
The HHSD will begin providing Non-Emergency Transport services, prevention services, and enhancing the community's access to behavioral health services.
The Council recognizes that future and existing services are considered "billable services" by third party payers including Medicaid and authorized Managed Healthcare Organizations.
The Council acknowledges the benefits to the Pueblo of engaging in third party billing for providing critical health services.

The Council acknowledges all Federal and State regulations associated with implementing third party billing, including the requirement to reinvest all third party revenue back into health programs for support and enhanced services as found in 25 U.S.C. § 5325(m)1.
The Governor is authorized to negotiate and execute contract agreements and to take any other actions necessary to effectuate a contract with the following Third Party Payers:

State Authorized Medicaid and Medicare programs
Presbyterian Health Care
Blue Cross Blue Shield
Molina Health Care
United Health Care
The Governor is authorized to determine whether, and to the extent, non-native clients may access the Pueblo's health services, if they are otherwise eligible.

The Governor is authorized and directed to develop and implement such finance and accounting policies, procedures, and reporting requirements specific to third party billing, necessary for the Pueblo's compliance with all State and Federal regulations, laws, and requirements. Governor shall develop the third party billing policies and procedures and present them to the Council for approval within thirty (30) days of enactment of this Act.
The Governor and Executive Branch are hereby authorized to establish any necessary positions/offices within the HHSD to support efficient and policy compliant third party billing operations.
The Governor is authorized to further pursue contracting all eligible IHS PSFAs that would result in enhanced health services to the Pueblo.
The Governor or his designated representative shall provide financial reports on third party billing progress as often as directed by Council.
